capture.cameraPose: IWER Meta Quest 3 default pose with query-gated panel visibility priority and controller dev UI visible

notes: The capture shows the emergency chest-pain station with patient, dialogue, clinical panel, and input panel in view, but the simplified actors and sparse room do not yet communicate a production clinical environment.
Actors are primitive capsule forms and the bed/monitor/equipment remain block-level proxies; this artifact supports layout iteration, not asset realism validation.

The query-gated panel visibility mode makes all three panels materially more readable than the prior rich-evidence capture, but the IWER controller overlays still crowd the lower input panel and the text remains too soft for a headset readability claim.
The app-authored controller affordances are intentionally hidden in this capture mode, so interaction readiness must still be assessed through separate IWER input probes and physical Quest tests.
Depth-prioritized panels reduce actor occlusion and improve adversarial screenshot review, but they are an evidence-capture composition rather than a normal user-facing spatial layout.
This artifact is explicitly limited to IWER emulation and visual QA iteration; it is not physical Quest foreground evidence, not headset readability proof, and not production runtime readiness.
